channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Gemini 3.5 Flash for Agentic Coding: A Claude Coder's Guide
item.description: This article was originally published on avinashsangle.com. Gemini 3.5 Flash is Google's new Flash-tier coding model, generally available since May 19, 2026. It scores 76.2% on Terminal-Bench 2.1...

item.title: Paper Reading Notes: [JEPA]
item.description: [Paper Notes] JEPA: Self-Supervised Learning from Images with a Joint-Embedding Predictive Architecture🔗 TL;DR: JEPA learns a a generalized semantic representation with less data pairs by...

item.title: Why I Open-Sourced Only Layer Zero
item.description: Why I Open-Sourced Only Layer Zero For the past months I’ve been building a deterministic infrastructure foundation in Rust. Today I decided to publicly expose only the lowest layer of that...

item.title: Shopify Changed heldBy From a String to an Object in 2025-01 — Your Query Still Returns 200 OK
item.description: Shopify ships a new GraphQL Admin API version every quarter. Most releases are additive. A few are not. The 2025-01 release is one of the "are not": it removed a handful of fields that thousands...

item.title: Claude Code Commands Beginner’s Handbook
item.description: A practical guide to every CLI command, flag, and in-session slash command you need to get productive with Claude Code fast. Think of this as the Claude Code command cheat sheet your future self...

item.title: AI/ML Research Digest — May 23, 2026
item.description: Extreme KV‑Cache Compression and Long‑Context Efficiency Static quantization is giving way to rotation‑based and context‑sensitive schemes. OCTOPUS and OScaR reach near‑lossless INT2 performance...

item.title: The Excel Paradox of Coding
item.description: Last week, for the nth time, I had to bulk-import records using Excel. No matter how advanced and complex your business rules and code, they often circle back to reading and writing Excel files. I...

item.title: AI is powerful. Snippets are instant.
item.description: It’s been a couple of years since the AI hype really took off and if you’re a professional software engineer like me, you’ve probably already found places where it genuinely improves your...

item.title: The Shared Cursor
item.description: An agent I built six months ago answered a question I didn't ask. It noticed the silence between two of my messages and offered a small observation: that I keep starting drafts on Mondays and...

item.title: Facts Without Sources Are Just Guesswork — Evidence-Linked Memory for Hermes Agent
item.description: Memory With Receipts: Evidence-Linked Recall for AI Agents Description: A local, no-model-required memory benchmark for AI agents where every extracted fact points back to the transcript event...

item.title: Welcome NVIDIA Cosmos 3: The First Open Omni-model for Physical AI Reasoning and Action
This is the Microsoft Surface Laptop Ultra with Nvidia RTX Spark
item.description: Once upon a time, Microsoft had to write off $900 million betting an Arm-based Nvidia chip could power its first flagship Windows portable, the original Microsoft Surface. But today, it's trying...

item.title: Nvidia announces RTX Spark as ‘the most efficient PC chip ever built’
item.description: This fall, Nvidia will officially become a consumer PC chipmaker like Intel, AMD, Apple, and Qualcomm, putting a complete computing chip - not just graphics - into the very heart of laptops and...

item.title: AMD’s new pitch: our old tech is so good you should just keep using it
item.description: Computex 2026 is underway in Taiwan, and we're expecting all manner of flashy computers with jaw-dropping prices (or no prices at all) as the entire industry navigates RAMageddon. But for desktop...

item.title: The QD-OLED gaming monitor that started it all got a big upgrade
item.description: Alienware is taking to this year's Computex 2026 in Taipei to announce some cool gaming monitors, most notably two exciting OLED options that are coming at different points this year. First off,...

item.title: Dell is bringing back the XPS 13 as a MacBook Neo competitor — with a temporary discount to $599
item.description: Dell is making good on its tease from CES and finally announcing a new XPS 13. The XPS 13 returns as a budget-friendly option, launching in July at a promotional student price of $599 - though...

item.title: Apple’s strategy for smart glasses is the same as smart watches
item.description: Apple isn't just looking to take on Meta in the smart glasses market; it's looking to upend eyewear as a whole, according to Bloomberg's Mark Gurman. When the Apple Watch launched, it wasn't...

item.title: Erin Brockovich takes aim at data center secrecy
item.description: Environmental activist Erin Brockovich has a new mission.

item.title: How to watch Nvidia’s Computex keynote
item.description: NVIDIA's CEO Jensen Huang is set to take the stage for his GTC Taipei keynote at 8PM PT / 11PM ET. You can watch all the announcements here and embedded below. Rumors have been flying about what...

item.title: Here’s your first look at ‘A Minecraft Movie Squared’ with Kirsten Dunst as Alex
item.description: The A Minecraft Movie sequel officially has a title: A Minecraft Movie Squared. What's more, we now know that Kirsten Dunst will star as Alex, the game's female character option, and that Matt...

item.title: Feeble Little Horse leans into digital weirdness on bitknot
item.description: From the opening moments of bitknot, it's obvious that Feeble Little Horse has found an entirely new gear. Where on Girl with Fish the blown-out textures were more '90s indie rock and shoegaze, on...

item.title: United flight forced to turn around because of a Bluetooth speaker name
item.description: United flight 236 from Newark to Palma de Mallorca on Saturday night was forced to turn around just an hour after takeoff due to security concerns around a Bluetooth signal. Multiple Redditors...

item.title: Making sense of the debate over AI psychosis
item.description: On the latest episode of Equity, we debate whether tech CEOs are "uniquely prone to AI psychosis."

item.title: Dutch Authorities Dismantle Botnet Linked to 17 Million Infected Devices
item.description: Dutch authorities have announced the takedown of a botnet that enslaved millions of infected devices, including computers, tablets, smartphones, and IoT devices, to carry out malicious attacks....

item.title: How Turkey Hacked the Hair Transplant Industry
item.description: From specialized motors to the use of machine learning algorithms, Turkey’s billion-dollar hair-transplant industry is the result of a constant process of innovation.

item.title: SoftBank says it will invest up to €75 billion to build French data centers
item.description: The goal, the firm said, is to develop and operate up to 5 gigawatts of additional data center capacity.

item.title: ‘What a joke’: Github Copilot’s new token-based billing spurs consternation among devs
item.description: The golden age of Microsoft's Github Copilot appears to be at an end.

item.title: Meta is reportedly developing an AI pendant
item.description: Meta seems to be making big bets on AI-powered hardware.

item.title: I put Google’s 24/7 AI assistant Gemini Spark to work, and it’s actually pretty useful
item.description: Gemini Spark helps automate everyday tasks, from inbox summaries to local event planning, but it’s unclear why Google made it a separate product.

item.title: As the browser wars heat up, here are the hottest alternatives to Chrome and Safari in 2026
item.description: We’ve compiled an overview of some of the top alternative browsers available today aiming to challenge Chrome and Safari.
